Plumes fall off with the square of the distance and they add together, so three neighbouring claims at 100% read, from a distance, exactly like a single claim at 300%. Their reach grows with the square root of the value: ten times the pollution carries about three times as far. A poisoned claim is never a private problem for long.
trans-unit.target: 一旦释放到土壤和地下水位中，地面污染物就不容易停止或清除。 《生态物语》中的各种材料都是地面污染的来源，最主要的是采矿产生的尾矿以及手工艺和其他玩家活动产生的垃圾。如果污染物的来源保持不变或增加，则污染会随着浓度的增加而逐渐增加。当污染物源与地面接触时，其污染会随着时间的流逝而扩散和消散。

trans-unit.source: Air Pollution
trans-unit.target: 空气污染

trans-unit.source:
Many player activities create harmful emissions which if released into the atmosphere act as air pollution. Crafting at objects with CO2 outputs or running generators with them contributes to local and global pollution levels. Over long periods of time, global CO2 levels contributes to a global warming effect which affects sea level in turn. There are natural sinks and intakes for some pollutants like CO2 such as forests.
Where a plume of air pollution meets rainfall, it comes back down as acid rain and feeds the Acid Rain layer of whatever ground lies beneath it. Emissions are therefore never only an air problem: an unfiltered smokestack slowly sours the land downwind of it.
trans-unit.target: 许多玩家的活动会产生有害的排放物，如果释放到大气中，则会产生空气污染。在具有二氧化碳输出的物体上进行手工制作或与它们一起运行发电机，都会造成局部和全球污染水平。在很长一段时间内，全球二氧化碳水平会导致全球变暖效应，进而影响海平面。有一些自然的汇和一些诸如二氧化碳之类的污染物的吸入口，例如森林。空气污染的浓度将以酸雨的形式回落到地面，我们使用空气污染和当地的降雨量进行模拟。

trans-unit.source: Water Pollution
trans-unit.target: 水污染

trans-unit.source:
Water holds no pollution of its own; it carries what the ground gives it. Where a plume reaches water it travels roughly three times further than it would over land, and arrives correspondingly diluted, a long weak tail drawn out across a lake or into the open sea. A tailings pile on a riverbank contaminates a great deal more than the riverbank.
Pipes are the other way water becomes a problem. An effluent released from an open pipe end poisons the ground where it lands, each fluid feeding its own layer: {SewageItem} fouls the soil, {IndustrialSewageItem} carries heavy metals, and {ChemicalWasteFluidItem} is caustic enough to be in a class of its own. Run them into a {WasteFilterItem} instead and they come out as {WaterItem}, clean enough to release or reuse, with the solids they carried captured for you to store or recycle.
Because open water only reflects its sources, there is nothing in it to scrub out directly. Contain or remove the sources on the shore, remediate the claims that feed it, and the water clears along with them.
trans-unit.target: 水是一种媒介，污染通过该媒介可以更快，更广泛地传播，向下游涌入并在湖泊或海洋中开花。水中的污染通常来自附近的地面污染源，但在空气污染严重的地区，也可能通过酸雨技工进入水中。去除水源是目前阻止水污染的唯一方法，但是清理和修复策略是我们未来考虑的功能。

trans-unit.source: Containment
trans-unit.target: 遏制

trans-unit.source:
Storing polluting material properly is the cheapest way to keep it out of the ground. A polluting block dumped in the open contributes its full amount, and so, be warned, does the same material sitting in an ordinary chest: only purpose-built containment holds anything back. A {GarbageStorageItem} keeps 90% of what it holds out of the soil, an {AdvancedGarbageStorageItem} 95%, and a {SealedGarbageStorageItem} 99%. None of them is perfect: a large enough dump will still poison the claim it stands on, however well it has been packed.
Producing less waste to begin with is the other half of the answer. A {GarbageSorterItem} separates mixed garbage into materials that can be recycled and put back to use, and a {ResidualWasteSorterItem} does the same for the residuals of industry, leaving a smaller volume of genuinely unusable waste behind. Space must still be made for what remains, and where that space goes should be a deliberate decision of your society rather than an accident of where the carts stopped.
trans-unit.target: 在许多情况下，没有完美的解决方案来处理污染物质。必须留出空间来存储各种污染物，并且必须以防止污染物重新进入周围环境的方式来存储污染物。这是对《生态物语》行业的环境管理至关重要的挑战。目前，尾矿力学需要在地下进行深层存储。随着进一步的发展，这将得到改善。遏制机制是我们要改进的功能的最前线，与《生态物语》中的污染功能组有关。玩家应该使用收容措施来处理其社会产生的大量尾矿和垃圾。

trans-unit.source: The introduction of harmful contaminates into the natural environment that can cause adverse changes.
trans-unit.target: 动植物的生存系统为人类的生存提供了手段。

trans-unit.source:
Constitutional Articles define how things happen in the government: who can pass laws, set taxes, change the rules, etc. It can also define certain actions as needing an election, by specifying an {Election Process|Election Processes} inside them.
Constitutional Articles can be added in two places. On a {CapitolItem}, you can add up to three, and on {AmendmentsItem} you can add up to another 10 more per item.
Defining the rules of the government is very important for keeping it a stable, society-supporting structure. Governments can be granted a great deal of power over the world with laws, and there's a danger of this power being abused. By setting a system of checks and balances, citizens can design a government that can undergo a number of changes, including changes of leadership, while maintaining the power to effect positive changes in the world.
trans-unit.target: 公约条款定义了公会中的事情的发生方式：谁可以通过章程，设定税率，更改规则等。它还可以通过在其中指定[Election Process|Election Processes] 来定义某些需要推选的行为。公约条款可以在两个地方添加。在[CapitolItem] 上，你最多可以添加三个，在[AmendmentsItem] 上，每个项目可以最多添加10个。定义公会规则对于保持公会稳定，支持社会的结构非常重要。可以通过章程授予公会在世界范围内的大量权力，并且存在滥用这种权力的危险。通过建立制衡机制，公民可以设计出可以进行许多变革的公会，包括领导层的变革，同时保持影响世界积极变革的力量。

trans-unit.source: A set of rules about how the government works.
trans-unit.target: 一套有关公会运作方式的规则。

trans-unit.source:
A constitution is a founding document defining how a society will be governed. A constitution must be created before any other government object. They are created at a {Capitol|CapitolItem}.
Ratifying a Constitution Once a capitol is placed, a constitution can be proposed by opening it and clicking Create Ratification Election Draft. You can add additional provisions ({Titles|Elected Titles}, {Election Processes}, etc.) to the election before starting voting. These provisions can then be referenced in the constitution draft.
Overthrowing Governments If there is already a constitution active, a new constitution can be proposed to take its place if the housing value of the new capitol room exceeds that of the existing capitol by a significant amount, depending on the server settings.
Note: A forthcoming update of Eco will allow multiple concurrent governments.
trans-unit.target: 公约是定义社会治理方式的基础文件。必须在任何其他公会反对之前制定公约。它们是在[Capitol|CapitolItem] 中创建的。颠覆公会设立公约台后，可以通过打开公约并单击“批准”来批准公约。只要没有其他公约，公会将立即成立。如果已经有一个有效的公约，则新的公约只能在其房间的房屋价值大大超过现有公约的情况下取而代之，具体取决于服务器设置。注意：即将发布的《生态物语》更新将允许多个并发公会。

trans-unit.source: The founding document for a government.
trans-unit.target: 公会成立文件。

trans-unit.source:
Demographics are classes of citizen, created in the government. These can then be used anywhere you can specify a citizen (permissions, laws, etc).
For example, citizens could define a demographic 'Lumberjacks', which would only contain users with a logging skill level of 3 or more. Then, a law could be created that only permits 'Lumberjacks' to cut down old-growth trees.
Demographics are defined at a {Census Bureau|CensusBureauItem}.
Demographics can also have wages set on them. Wages are paid periodically as long as a citizen remains within the demographic.
trans-unit.target: 群体资料是在公会中创建的公民阶层。然后可以在你可以指定公民的任何地方（权限，章程等）使用这些功能。例如，公民可以定义群体的“伐木工人”，其中只会包含伐木技能水平为3或更高的用户。然后，可以制定仅允许“伐木工人”砍伐老树的章程。群体信息在[Census Bureau|CensusBureauItem] 中定义。群体也可以设定工资。只要公民仍在人口中，便定期支付工资。

trans-unit.source: Defines a set of citizens matching some criteria.
trans-unit.target: 定义一组符合某些条件的公民。

trans-unit.source:
Districts are created by players at a {ZoningOfficeItem} and can be used as areas that are specified by laws.
A district is a selected area of land, non-contiguous, which players can adjust at will. Districts are contained in District Maps, and there can be multiple district maps in the world. However, within each district map, no districts may overlap.
Districts can be used to designate where actions are allowed with laws. A law can be created, for example, players can create a law that prevents chopping trees in a 'Protected Zone' district.
trans-unit.target: 区域是由玩家在[ZoningOfficeItem] 创建的，可以用作章程规定的区域。地区是选定的土地区域，不连续，玩家可以随意调整。地区包含在“地区地图”中，世界上可以有多个地区地图。但是，在每个地区地图中，任何地区都不能重叠。可以使用地区来指定章程允许采取行动的地方。例如，可以制定章程，以防止在“保护区”区域创建砍伐树木。

trans-unit.source: A defined section of land, which can be referenced by laws and other systems.
trans-unit.target: 定义的土地部分，章程和其他系统可以参考。

trans-unit.source:
Elected Titles are government officials that can be assigned permissions to manage government policy. These titles can be used anywhere you can specify a citizen (permissions, laws, etc.).
Elected titles are created at a {GovernmentOfficeItem}. Several conditions can be set when defining an elected title:
- An {Election Process|Election Processes} with term limits for title holders - The number of citizens who can hold the elected title at the same time - Requirements to stay in office once elected to the title - A successor who takes over the position if a title holder is removed from office
While creating an elected title, the following permissions can be assigned:
- Setting the tax rate at a {TreasuryItem} - Access to spend the funds in the Treasury bank account - Government banking privileges to create government bank accounts that are exempt from personal wealth calculations - The ability to place a global map marker to notify citizens of a point of interest
Elected titles can be assigned wages that are paid periodically to the citizens that hold the title.
trans-unit.target: 当选头衔是公会要职，可以分配其管理公会政策的权限。这些头衔可在你可以指定公民的任何地方使用（权限，章程等）。选择的头衔在[GovernmentOfficeItem] 中创建。定义当选头衔时可以设置几个条件：-拥有头衔持有人任期限制的[Election Process|Election Processes]-可以同时拥有当选头衔的公民数量-一旦当选为职务-如果职务持有人被免职，将接任头衔的继任人在创建当选职务时，可以分配以下权限：-将税率设置为[TreasuryItem]-可以将资金用于金库银行账户-建立免于个人财富计算的公会银行账户的公会银行特权-可以放置全局地图标记以通知公民感兴趣的点的功能可以为当选头衔分配工资，并定期向对应头衔的公民支付工资。

trans-unit.source: Elected Titles are government officials that can be assigned permissions to manage government policy.
trans-unit.target: 当选头衔是公会要职，可以分配其管理公会政策的权限。

trans-unit.source:
Citizens can define new types of elections at a {BoardOfElectionsItem}, and each election process created there can be used for various changes to government and leadership.
{Civic Articles} can reference Election Processes in order to designate actions that require an election. {Elected Titles} created at a {GovernmentOfficeItem} must specify an Election Process by which new members get elected.
Each Election Process can define a different set of voters, vetoers, timings, percentage needed to win, etc. Using different types of elections, citizens can design their government to have different seats of power. For example, they can define a powerful Title that must be elected by the general population but can be impeached by them as well.
trans-unit.target: 公民可以在[BoardOfElectionsItem] 上定义新的推选类型，并且在那里创建的每个推选过程都可以用于公会和领导层的各种变更。 [Civic Articles] 可以引用推选过程，以指定需要进行推选的行动。在[GovernmentOfficeItem] 创建的[Elected Titles] 必须指定一个推选程序，通过该程序可以推选新成员。每个推选过程可以定义一组不同的选民，否决权，时间，获胜所需的百分比等。通过使用不同类型的推选，公民可以设计其公会具有不同的权力所在地。例如，他们可以定义一个强大的头衔，该头衔必须由一般人群推选产生，但也可以由他们弹劾。

trans-unit.source: Defines how an election takes place.
trans-unit.target: 定义推选的方式。

trans-unit.source:
Elections can take two forms: - Elections of Officials. These are elections of {Elected Titles} which give specific powers to a citizen. - Referendums. A yes-or-no vote that will determine if a specific civic action should be taken.
Elections of Officials are started at a {BallotBoxItem}, where citizens can also perform a number of other civic actions. Depending on what {Civic Articles} are available, these actions may or may not require an election; if they do, the citizen will have the option to start one immediately.
Referendums are started at the various government buildings, each corresponding to a different government action. These include things like creating a new {Law|Laws} or performing an {Executive Action|Executive Actions}. When proposing an election of this type, multiple provisions can be added to one election package that will be part of the same yes-or-no vote.
Elections can have a number of settings determined by their {Election Process|Election Processes}. This will state who can vote, who can veto, how long the election lasts, what percent is needed to win, etc. Multiple types of elections can be created for different purposes. For example, a representative government could be created with some elections only allowing elected officials to vote, while most citizens vote only in elections for representatives.
trans-unit.target: 推选可以采用两种形式：-官员推选。这些是[Elected Titles] 的推选，赋予公民特定的权力。 -全民公决。是或否表决，将决定是否应采取特定的公民行动。推选从[BallotBoxItem] 开始，公民可以在其中执行许多公民行为。根据可用的[Civic Articles]，这些行动可能会或可能不需要推选；如果他们这样做，公民将可以选择立即开始。推选可以具有根据其[Election Process|Election Processes] 确定的许多设置。这将说明谁可以投票，谁可以否决权，推选能持续多长时间，赢得推选所需的百分比等。可以为不同的目的创建多种类型的推选。例如，可以创建一个代议制公会，其中一些推选仅允许民选官员投票，而大多数公民仅在代表推选中投票。

trans-unit.source: A poll set to a group of citizens to determine a position or referendum.
trans-unit.target: 对一组公民进行的民意测验，以确定头衔或全民投票。

执行行动是一次性行动，可以一次影响特定群体的公民或所有公民。可以使用[Civic Action Component] 在[ExecutiveOfficeItem] 上提出执行动作。这些操作具有三个主要效果：-一次性发放[Tax|Taxes]-通过[TreasuryItem] 向公民付款-更改现有土地的[Property] 所有者根据可用的[Civic Articles]，行动可能需要也可能不需要推选。一些民事条款指出，只有[Elected Titles] 才可以执行行政行为。

trans-unit.source: An action performed by a government official having effects on taxes, property, etc.
trans-unit.target: 由公会官员执行的对税收，土地等产生影响的行动。

trans-unit.source: "The punishment which the wise suffer who refuse to take part in the government, is to live under the government of worse men."\n- Plato
trans-unit.target: 拒绝参与政治的惩罚，就是被糟糕的人统治。“\n-柏拉图”

trans-unit.source: Between the ecosystem and the economy lies the government, run by citizens to ensure the survival and thriving of both.
trans-unit.target: 公会位于生态系统和经济之间，由公民管理，以确保两者的生存和繁荣。

trans-unit.source: The government is created by citizens, piece by piece, growing their power and capability alongside the power of the populace. The parts of the government are:
trans-unit.target: 公会是由公民逐步建立的，其权力和能力与人民的力量一起增长。公会部门是:

trans-unit.source: {Constitution} Before any other aspect of government can be created, a Constitution must be created at a {CapitolItem}. It determines how the government operates via civic articles.
trans-unit.target: [Constitution] 在建立公会的任何其他方面之前，必须在[CapitolItem] 处创建公约。它决定了公会如何通过公民条款运作。

trans-unit.source: {Civic Articles} A constitution contains civic articles which define how the government functions: who can do what, and how. They can be created at a {CapitolItem} on a new constitution or added to an existing constitution at an {AmendmentsItem} table.
trans-unit.target: [Civic Articles] 公约包含公民条款，这些条款定义了公会的职能：谁可以做什么，以及如何做。它们可以在新公约的[CapitolItem] 中创建，也可以在[AmendmentsItem] 表中添加到现有公约中。

trans-unit.source: {Laws} Laws determine rules and processes that all citizens are compelled to follow. A {SmallCourtItem} can hold up to 2 laws and a {LargeCourtItem} up to 5.
trans-unit.target: [Laws] 章程决定了所有公民都必须遵守的规则和程序。 [CourtItem] 最多可容纳3条章程。

trans-unit.source: {Elections} Elections will start automatically when making changes that require them, as determined by the constitution. They can have many effects (changing laws, electing leaders, performing executive actions).
trans-unit.target: [Elections] 根据公约的规定，需要进行更改时，推选将自动开始。它们可以产生许多影响（更改章程，推选领导人，执行行政措施）。

trans-unit.source: {Election Processes} An election process determines how elections work: who can vote and veto, how long they last, what majorities are required, etc. A {BoardOfElectionsItem} can hold up to three election processes.
trans-unit.target: [Election Processes] 推选过程决定推选的工作方式：谁可以投票和否决票，推选能持续多长时间，需要多数票等，一个[BoardOfElectionsItem] 最多可以容纳三个推选过程。

trans-unit.source: {Elected Titles} An Elected Title is a government position that can be given special privileges. A {GovernmentOfficeItem} can hold one elected title.
trans-unit.target: [Elected Titles] 当选头衔是可以赋予特殊特权的公会头衔。 [GovernmentOfficeItem] 可以保留一个当选头衔。

trans-unit.source: {Demographics} A demographic defines a group of people according to specific rules. This group can then be used in other aspects of the government. A {CensusBureauItem} can hold up to three demographics.
trans-unit.target: [Demographics] 受众特征是根据特定规则定义的一群人。然后可以将该组用于公会的其他方面。 [CensusBureauItem] 最多可容纳三个受众特征。

trans-unit.source: {Districts} Defines an area of land which can be referenced in laws and other places. A {ZoningOfficeItem} can hold up to three district maps.
trans-unit.target: [Districts] 定义了章程和其他地方可以参考的土地区域。 [ZoningOfficeItem] 最多可容纳三张地区地图。

trans-unit.source: {Executive Actions} An executive action is a one-time operation that can be performed to enact changes to society. They are performed at an {ExecutiveOfficeItem} by users given privileges from the constitution (typically elected titles).
trans-unit.target: [Executive Actions] 行政行为是可以执行一次以改变社会的一次性行动。由公约赋予特权的用户在[ExecutiveOfficeItem] 中执行（通常是推选产生的头衔）。

trans-unit.source: {Ballot Boxes|Elections} A ballot box allows operations to be performed related to elections and holding office.
trans-unit.target: [Ballot Boxes|Civic Action Component] 投票箱允许执行与推选和担任职务有关的操作。

trans-unit.source: {Treasury|Treasury Component} Determines taxes on various parts of the economy.
trans-unit.target: [Treasury|Treasury Component] 确定经济各个部分的税金。

trans-unit.source: An institution designed and run by the people to promote the common good.
trans-unit.target: 由人民设计和经营以促进共同利益的机构。

trans-unit.source:
Laws are the means by which the government makes changes in the world, to achieve the goals of the populace.
Laws are created by placing a {SmallCourtItem} or a {LargeCourtItem}, and depending on the available {Civic Articles}, may require an election to pass. Each law is created with a list of causes and effects, and when the cause happens, the effect will be activated. This allows a lot of flexibility in how the laws work, and they can cover many areas, from taxation to regulation to paid out funds incentivizing activities.
Many ‘Cause’ choices will provide context about the citizen who triggered it, where it was triggered, etc. These ‘context’ properties can be seen in the Cause tooltip, and are available for specifying data in the ‘Effects’. For example, an effect may tax the specific player who chopped down a tree.
trans-unit.target: 章程是公会改变世界以实现民众目标的手段。通过放置[CourtItem] 来创建章程，根据可用的[Civic Articles] 可能需要通过推选。每条定律都创建有因果列表，并且当因果发生时，将激活效应。这为章程的运作方式提供了很大的灵活性，并且它们可以涵盖许多领域。从税收到监管再到支付资金激励活动。许多“原因”选项将提供有关触发它的公民，触发它的位置等方面的环境。这些“环境”属性可以在“原因”提示信息中看到，并且可用于在“效果”中指定数据。例如，效果可能会使砍伐树木的特定玩家负担重。

trans-unit.source: Cause-and-Effect rules designed by citizens and enforced automatically in order to achieve societal goals.
trans-unit.target: 公民设计的因果规则，并自动执行以实现社会目标。

trans-unit.source:
For the good of the people, or at least the enrichment of the government, taxes can be levied on many actions in Eco.
Treasury Taxes At a {TreasuryItem}, Taxes can be set on a variety of actions: - Sales Tax. Tax applied to the sale of goods at a store. - Crafting Fee Tax. Tax applied to the fees taken from sharing your crafting station. - Contract Tax. Taxes taken from payments made through contracts and work parties. - Direct Transfer Tax. Taxes taken from transfers between players. - Self Direct Transfer Tax. Taxes taken when transferring to an account you have access to (to optionally protect against tax avoidance loopholes).
Only a citizen holding an {Elected Title|Elected Titles} who has been granted the right to ‘Set Taxes at the Treasury’ may adjust these rates. All taxes taken from these settings will be stored in the special ‘Treasury’ bank account. Allocation may be made out of this special bank account into others through commands on the treasury.
Law Taxes Taxes can also be set through {Laws}, which can have their amounts and destinations determined dynamically. The bank account they are placed in can also be determined, allowing funding for specific purposes, stored in bank accounts accessible to a limited number of people only. Using the law system, these taxes can be very specifically targeted and adjusted based on the actions that are happening.
For example, a 'Minister of Transportation' Elected Title position could be created, having sole access to a Transportation Fund {Bank Account|Bank Accounts}. A law could then be created to tax the production and trade of fuel, with proceeds going into the Transportation Fund, where the currently elected Minister of Transportation can use them to fund new roadbuilding {Work Parties}.
trans-unit.target: 为了人民的利益，或者至少是为了公会的富裕，可以对《生态物语》中的许多行为征收税款。金库税在[TreasuryItem] 中，可以对多种操作设置税收：-销售税。在商店销售商品时应缴纳的税款。 -手工艺费税。共享你的手工制作站所收取的费用应缴税。 -合同税。从通过合同和工作方支付的款项中收取的税款。 -直接转让税。玩家之间的转会收取的税款。 -自直接转移税。转帐至你有权使用的帐户时所收取的税款（以选择避免避税漏洞）。只有持有[Elected Title|Elected Titles] 的公民被授予了“在金库设置税”的权利，才可以调整这些税率。从这些设置中收取的所有税费都将存储在特殊的“金库”银行账户中。可以通过财政部的指令将这个特殊的银行账户中的资金分配到其他帐户中。章程税也可以通过[Laws] 设置税，可以动态确定税额和目的地。还可以确定它们所存放的银行账户，以便为特定目的的特定资金提供资金，并存储在只能供少数人使用的银行账户中。使用章程制度，可以根据正在发生的行动非常明确地确定和调整这些税收。例如，可以创建“交通部长”推选头衔头衔，并且可以直接访问交通基金[Bank Account|Bank Accounts]。然后可以制定一项章程，对燃料的生产和贸易征税，并将所得款项划入运输基金，由当选的运输部长可以将其用于资助新的道路建设[Work Parties]。

trans-unit.source: Tax can be taken from the economy to fund publics works project as well as incentivize or disincentivize economic activity.
trans-unit.target: 可以从经济中收取税款，以资助公共工程项目以及激励或消灭经济活动。

trans-unit.source:
Titles are used for designating a group of users, then referencing that group in other things like permissions. They are different from {Demographics} in that Titles are specified directly by citizens, while the citizens within a demographic are automatically defined based on a citizen-defined condition.
There are two types of Titles available:
- Regular Titles. These can be created and managed by anyone at a {RegistrarItem}. There, the citizen can set which other citizens can assign the role (managers) and which are occupying the role. - Elected Titles. These titles can only be elected (or appointed) and are created at a {GovernmentOfficeItem}. The properties of the government office determine what kind of {Election} is needed to elect the given position. Elected Titles can only be left if they are forcibly removed from office, if they resign (both performed at a {BallotBoxItem}), or if their term ends as defined in the position’s term limits.
Elected Titles give a lot of flexibility in defining the governance of a world, and privileges can be tied directly to these accounts. For example, a tax law could be created that taxes vehicle usage and puts it into a fund that only the Elected Title ‘Minister of Transportation’ has access to. If there is another election for that position and it changes leaders, then the former citizen will automatically lose access to the account.
By setting requirements on the Elected Title, you can ensure the continued good governance by citizens holding office. For example, you could place a requirement that all occupants of a given office are part of the 'Active' demographic may hold it. If an office holder becomes inactive, then they will automatically be removed from office and a specified successor put in their position.
Titles can also have wages set on them. Wages are paid periodically as long as a citizen continues to occupy the title (and the payer has funds).
trans-unit.target: 头衔用于指定一组用户，然后以其他方式（例如权限）引用该组。它们与[Demographics] 的不同之处在于，头衔由公民直接指定，而群体中的公民是根据公民定义的条件自动定义的。有两种类型的头衔：-常规头衔。可由[RegistrarItem] 的任何人创建和管理这些文件。在那里，公民可以设置哪些其他公民可以分配角色（经理）以及哪些人正在担任角色。 -当选头衔。这些头衔只能推选（或任命）并在[GovernmentOfficeItem] 中创建。公会部门的属性决定了推选给定头衔需要哪种[Election]。仅当被强制离职，辞职（在[BallotBoxItem] 上执行，或者其任期在该头衔的任期限制中定义）时，才可以保留当选头衔。当选头衔为定义治理提供了很大的灵活性可以将特权直接绑定到这些帐户，例如，可以制定一项税法，对车辆的使用情况征税，并将其放入只有当选“交通部长”才能使用的基金。再次推选该头衔并更改领导者，那么前任公民将自动失去对帐户的访问权限。通过对“当选头衔”设置要求，可以确保任职公民继续保持良好的治理。例如，你可以提出要求某个办公室的所有人员都是“活跃”人口的一部分，如果某个办公室的持有人变得不活跃，他们将自动从办公室撤职，并指定一个接班人他们的位置。头衔上也可以设置工资。只要公民继续担任头衔（并且付款人拥有资金），便会定期支付工资。

trans-unit.source: Positions which can hold one or more citizens. Used for designating permissions and paying wages.
trans-unit.target: 可以容纳一个或多个公民的头衔。用于指定许可和支付工资。

trans-unit.source:
A citizen cannot thrive without proper shelter, and together with your {Nutrition} your Housing value will determine your {Experience} multiplier. The better your house’s materials and furnishing are, the higher your experience multiplier.
Additionally, many objects require houses and utilities to operate. The housing system consists of the following parts: - {Residency}. You can share residency of a property with others. - {Rooms}. A room is a fully contained space, and many objects require being placed inside a room to function. - {Property}. Citizens can claim individual plots of land which they then can control access to. - {Power}. Many items require a power source to operate, and generated power can be shared among many citizens. - {Sewage}. Certain items require a sewage system of pipes, pumps, and wastewater treatment.
trans-unit.target:
没有合适的住房任何市民都无法打拼出成绩，你的住房点和[Nutrition] 一起决定了[Experience] 的系数。房子建材和装潢越好，经验系数越大。
另外，许多物品需要房子和设施足够才能运作。整个住房体系有下列部分组成： - [Residency]。你可以与其他人共住一处房产。 - [Rooms]。房间是完全室内的空间，许多物品都需要在房间内才能工作。 - [Property]。市民可以认领独立的地块，自行设置准入权限。 - [Power]。许多物品需要能源来运转，产出的能源可以在许多市民之间共享。 - [Sewage]。特定物品需要由管道，水泵和废水处理单元租车过的污水处理系统。

trans-unit.source: Your housing (along with your diet) determines your skill gain.
trans-unit.target: 你的住房（以及饮食）决定了你的技能提升。

trans-unit.source:
Many objects require power to function, and as your society progresses the types of power will progress as well.
Early on, most power will come from calories expended by citizens. Local-power sources can also be built such as {Waterwheels|Waterwheel Item}, powering only the immediate vicinity. When technology progresses to electricity, power can then be generated centrally, allowing groups to form together to power towns and cities as a group rather than having to individually setup power sources (community projects like this are a great fit for using the {Taxes} system to fund them, and {Contracts} and {Work Parties} to get them built).
Many types of power generation exist, and each has different fuel needs as well as environmental impact. The building, fueling, and operation of power plants can become a major {Pollution} source as technology advances, and citizens will need to carefully regulate the types of power used and how pollution is handled to prevent an ecological catastrophe.
trans-unit.target: 许多物体需要权力才能发挥作用，随着社会的进步，权力的类型也会随之发展。早期，大多数能量将来自公民消耗的卡路里。也可以构建本地电源，例如[Waterwheels|Waterwheel Item]，仅向附近地区供电。当技术发展为电力时，便可以集中产生电力，从而使各团体可以一起为城镇供电，而不必单独设置电源（这样的社区项目非常适合使用[Taxes] 系统为他们提供资金，以及[Contracts] 和[Work Parties] 来建立它们。存在多种类型的发电，每种都有不同的燃料需求以及对环境的影响。随着技术的进步，发电厂的建设，加油和运营可能成为主要的[Pollution] 来源，公民将需要仔细调节使用的电力类型以及如何处理污染以防止生态灾难。

trans-unit.source: Many objects require power to function, which can be generated in a variety of ways.
trans-unit.target: 许多物体需要动力才能发挥作用，可以通过多种方式产生动力。

trans-unit.source: By claiming Property, citizens can protect access to plots of land, determining who is allowed to access the land and what they are allowed to do on objects contained on it.
trans-unit.target: 通过认领土地，公民可以保护对土地的访问权，确定允许谁访问该土地以及允许他们对其中包含的物体执行什么操作。

trans-unit.source: Land Property Rights
trans-unit.target: 土地产权

trans-unit.source: Land can be claimed as property by using a {Land Claim Stake|ClaimToolItem} and targeting an unclaimed plot of land. When targeted, a {Settlement Claim Stake|SettlementProperty} will be planted, and the citizen will become the owner on the deed. The property at your current position can be seen in the world panel in the bottom right of the screen, and clicking the title will open the deed.
trans-unit.target: 通过使用[Land Claim Stake|Property Tool Item] 并以无人认领的土地为目标，可以将土地认领为土地。当定为目标时，将消耗[Land Claim Paper|Property Claim Item]，公民将成为契据的所有者。你当前位置的土地可以在屏幕右下方的世界面板中看到，单击头衔将打开契据。

trans-unit.source: Object Property Rights
trans-unit.target: 对象土地权

trans-unit.source: Objects can have their access rights set separately from the land they are on, to allow consumer access to objects that are on private land. These settings are determined in an {Authorization Component}.
trans-unit.target: 可以将对象的访问权与所在土地分开设置，以允许消费者访问私有土地上的对象。这些设置在[Authorization Component] 中确定。

trans-unit.source: Deeds
trans-unit.target: 契据

trans-unit.source: On the deed, citizens can add and remove parties that may access the land (place or remove blocks). The owner can also be changed by clicking the gear beside the owner name. Note that both {Demographics} and {Titles} can both be given ownership on a deed, including {Elected Titles}. Structuring a government this way can allow only elected officials to have certain access rights to land.
trans-unit.target: 根据契据，公民可以添加和删除可能访问该土地的各方（放置或删除街区）。也可以通过单击所有者名称旁边的齿轮来更改所有者。请注意，[Demographics]和[Titles]都可以拥有契据的所有权，包括[Elected Titles]。以这种方式组建公会可以只允许民选官员拥有某些土地使用权。

trans-unit.source: Citizens and governments may own property and determine the access rights of them.
trans-unit.target: 公民和公会可以拥有土地并确定他们的使用权。

trans-unit.source:
Rooms are enclosed areas protected from the elements where all holes to the outside world are filled with walls, doors and windows.
You can see the details about a room in a panel on the right side of the screen while you’re inside one stating its information. Many types of objects have Room Requirements and will take up space in their room, and require the materials of the room to be a specific quality. Your housing value is also affected by the quality of the room materials. This means a brick house for example will provide much more benefit than a dirt hovel.
Furnishings and Room Types By claiming land and building rooms on it, you will begin to gain additional experience, which persists even when offline. Adding additional types of rooms and furnishings for those rooms will increase it further. Each type of furniture is associated with a category, listed below. The room type will be determined by whichever category you have the highest contribution of furnishings from. Each room will have its value calculated by summing all the furnishings inside of it.
Repeats of the same furniture inside a room or repeats of the same room type in a house will give you diminishing returns; that is, you will still get housing points for them, but not as many as the first. Eventually the point gain will be negligible, and it won’t be worth adding any more of that type of furniture or room to the house. For example, your 12th bathroom is going to add much less value to your house than your first one.
Different types of properties can support different types of rooms, for example a 'Bedroom' is useful on 'Residence' property, but not a 'Cultural' property, while a 'Bathroom' is useful in both. Some room categories can have other types of furniture supporting them ('Cultural' furnishings can give value to a 'Living Room' for example), and this support value is capped to a specific percent of the main furniture (ie, the value of decorations in your bathroom is worthless if there's no toilet).
Some furnishings are considered support-categories only, and cannot have their own dedicated room. IE, Decoration furnishings can support any room, but cannot be a room category itself. Some room types have their value capped relative to the primary rooms of the house. IE, bathrooms are capped by default to 33% of the primary rooms of the house (so that a property that is just one large bathroom won't have any positive value, but if there are other rooms present it will).
Room Material Quality The average tier of the room material will also determine your housing value, by setting a soft cap. Once your room value has exceeded the soft cap for the room, then you will get diminishing returns for additional housing points. When this happens, it may be time to renovate the materials of the room.
trans-unit.target: 房间是封闭的区域，不受外界影响，在这些外部环境中，所有墙壁，门和窗户都充满了漏洞。当你进入一个房间时，你可以在屏幕右侧的面板中看到有关该房间的详细信息。许多类型的对象都有“房间要求”，并且会占用其房间中的空间，并且要求房间的材料具有特定的质量。你的房屋价值还受到房间材料质量的影响。举例来说，这意味着用砖砌房屋将比带泥棚提供更多收益。陈设和房间类型通过在上面拥有土地和建筑物的房间，你将开始获得额外的经验，即使在离线状态下，这种经验仍然存在。添加其他类型的房间和这些房间的家具将进一步增加它。每种家具都与一种房间相关联：浴室，卧室，厨房或普通房（显示在该项目的提示信息上）。房间类型将由你对家具陈设贡献最大的类别决定。每个房间的值将通过汇总房间内的所有家具来计算。在房间内重复使用相同的家具或在房子中重复使用相同类型的房间会使你的收益递减；也就是说，你仍然可以获得他们的住房积分，但没有第一住房积分那么多。最终，积分的增长将是微不足道的，并且在房屋中增加任何类型的家具或房间都是不值得的。例如，你的第12间浴室将为你的房屋增加比第一个浴室少的价值。房间材料的质量房间材料的平均等级还将通过设置软上限来确定你的房屋价值。一旦你的房间价值超过该房间的软上限，那么你获得的额外住房收益将递减。发生这种情况时，可能是时候装修房间的材料了。

trans-unit.source: Rooms protect you and your equipment from the elements, providing skill points and allowing equipment to function.
trans-unit.target: 房间可保护你和你的设备不受元素影响，提供技能点并允许设备运行。

trans-unit.source:
Many industrial and housing objects require sewage support to function. Typically, this means requiring water as an input, and an output for waste water. Every object that has a {Pipe Component} will need a sewage connection.
Sewage systems are created by connecting a {MechanicalWaterPumpItem} or {ElectricWaterPumpItem} to a water source, and then building Pipes between the different objects that need supplying. On the waste side, pipes will take the waste water from the object to either dump it into the environment (producing {Pollution} at the exit point), or process it at a {Waster Filter|WasteFilterItem}, which will generate {Compost|CompostItem} from the wastewater.
Pipes will automatically connect to any adjacent pipe block of the same type. To make sewage systems with different pipes in close proximity, use different materials of pipes.
trans-unit.target: 许多工业和住房物体需要污水支持才能发挥作用。通常，这意味着需要水作为废水的输入和输出。每个具有[Pipe Component] 的对象都需要污水连接。通过将[MechanicalWaterPumpItem] 或[ElectricWaterPumpItem] 连接到水源，然后在需要供水的不同对象之间建立管道来创建污水处理系统。在废物方面，管道会从物体中吸收废水，将其倾倒到环境中（在出口处产生[Pollution]），或在[Waster Filter|WasteFilterItem] 中进行处理，从而从废水中产生[Compost|CompostItem]。管道将自动连接到任何相同类型的相邻管道块。要使污水处理系统的各个管道紧挨着，请使用不同的管道材料。

trans-unit.source: Certain objects require sewage systems to supply water and remove wastewater.
trans-unit.target: 某些物品需要污水来供水和清除废水。

trans-unit.source: Components related to industry.
trans-unit.target: 与行业相关的组件。

trans-unit.source:
As you gain experience in Eco you will be able to unlock new specialties and new abilities within those specialties. There are two types of experience you will gain, Character Experience and Specialty Experience. Both are increased by your Experience Multiplier.
Character Experience Character Experience is your background learning rate, how fast you are gaining knowledge and abilities. It is constantly going up, even when you’re offline. Each real-day, you will gain Character Experience at the rate of 1 * your experience multiplier. When you reach a level up, you will gain a new specialty unlock, represented by a star. Each level up will require more and more character experience to gain, so it is very difficult to have more than a few specialties.
Specialty Experience Specialty Experience is your skill at a specific ability, and rather than going up constantly, it only goes up when you perform actions related to that specialty, such as crafting or performing labor. Like Character Experience, it is also multiplied by your Experience Multiplier. Along the left side of the screen, you’ll see icons displaying how much experience you’re gaining when you perform actions, and you can click these to get a closer look at the level ups for that specialty. Some level ups will allow you to unlock talents, which give specific bonuses related to the skills they’re in.
Experience Multiplier Your Experience Multiplier (or XP Multiplier) is a personal value representing your health and support system, and it determines how fast you learn new skills. It is determined as a sum of your {Nutrition} and {Housing|Housing Overview} values. Because it affects both your character and specialty experience points, you’ll want to keep it as high as possible by eating the nutritious food and having a well-furnished house.
trans-unit.target: 随着你在《生态物语》方面的经验积累，你将能够解锁新的专业和这些专业中的新功能。你将获得两种类型的体验：角色体验和专业体验。两者都会通过你的经验系数增加。角色体验是你的背景学习速度，即你获得知识和能力的速度。即使你处于离线状态，它也在不断上升。每天，你将以1 *经验系数的速率获得角色经验。升级后，你将获得一个新的特殊解锁，以星号表示。每个级别的提升都将需要越来越多的角色经验，因此很难拥有多个专业。专业经验专业经验是指你具有特定能力的技能，而不是不断提高，它仅在你执行与该专业相关的动作（例如手工艺或从事劳动）时才会提高。像角色体验一样，它也会与你的经验系数相乘。在屏幕的左侧，你将看到一些图标，这些图标显示了你执行操作时获得的经验，并且你可以单击这些图标来仔细查看该专业的水平提升。某些级别的提升将使你能够释放才华，这些才华与他们所掌握的技能有关，从而获得特定的奖励。经验系数是代表你的健康和支持系统的个人价值，它决定了你的速度如何学习新技能。它是你的[Nutrition] 和[Housing|Housing Overview] 值的总和。因为它会影响你的性格和专业经验点，所以你需要通过吃营养丰富的食物和布置整齐的房屋来保持尽可能高的水平。

trans-unit.source: There are two kinds of experience in Eco: Character Experience (gained by food and housing) and Specialty Experience (gained by performing actions in a specialty).
trans-unit.target: 生态学有两种经验：品格体验（通过食物和住房获得）和专业体验（通过在专业中进行活动获得）。

trans-unit.source:
Your {Experience} is determined by your nutrition and {Housing|Housing Overview}, and the more nutritious the food you eat the faster you will gain experience. The healthier you are, the faster you advance.
There are four nutrients represented in foods: Protein, Fat, Vitamins, and Carbs. Your experience multiplier is determined by adding up the average of each of these nutrients in all the food you’ve eaten in the last 24 hours. This means that if you eat junk food (low nutrients) it will affect you for 24 hours before it has passed through your body.
Additionally, a balance multiplier is applied, giving an additional 0% - 50% of your total based on how equal the nutrients are in your body. If you have exactly 25% of each nutrient for example, you will gain an extra 50% skill.
Depending on the difficulty settings for this server, a ‘skill gain multiplier’ may also be set which increases it further.
Finally, a base skill gain value is added to the total. This ensures that even when you’re totally starving, you’re still gaining at least some skill. You can see all these calculations for yourself in the tooltip for food in the bottom left UI.
Calories Calories, unlike nutrients, are spent when you perform work, and when you run out of calories you’ll be unable to work until you eat more. Calorie usage can be greatly reduced by upgraded tools and leveling-up skills. All food provides both calories and nutrients, so while gaining calories from food you’re also determining your skill multiplier, so watch that diet.
trans-unit.target: 你的经验[Experience] 取决于你的营养和住房[Housing|Housing Overview]，而你所吃的食物营养越丰富，你获得的[Experience] 就越快。你越健康，进步就越快。食物中含有四种营养素：蛋白质，脂肪，维生素和碳水化合物。通过将你过去24小时内食用的所有食物中每种营养素的平均值相加，可以确定你的经验系数。这意味着，如果你吃垃圾食品（营养物质含量低），它将在你通过身体之前24小时受到影响。此外，还会应用平衡系数，根据你体内营养的平衡程度，再增加总计的0％-50％。例如，如果每种营养素的含量准确地占25％，你将获得50％的额外技能。根据此服务器的难度设置，还可以设置一个“技能增益倍增器”，以使其进一步提高。最后，将基本技能增益值添加到总数中。这样可以确保即使你完全饿了，也至少会获得一些技能。你可以在左下角用户界面的食物提示中查看所有这些计算。卡路里不同于营养素，卡路里是在工作时消耗的，卡路里消耗殆尽时，你只有吃得更多才能工作。升级工具和升级技巧可以大大减少卡路里的使用。所有食物都提供卡路里和营养，因此在从食物中获取卡路里的同时，你还需要确定自己的技能系数，因此请注意饮食。养活世界粮食是生态经济中不断发展的社会的骨干力量，推动了建立文明所需的技能的增长。从农民到厨师的组织良好的食品生产者网络可确保公民以所需的速度获得技能。

trans-unit.source: Nutrition, alongside housing, determines how fast you gain experience.
trans-unit.target: 营养和住房决定了你获得经验的速度。

trans-unit.source:
Central to a thriving economy is the ability to specialize and trade, thereby working more efficiently than if you attempted to do everything yourself. Achieving a successful society depends on successfully accomplishing both.
A citizen's skills give them unique abilities and enhancements, which allows them to {Trade} with other citizens having different skills in the economy. As you gain {Experience}, you can unlock new {Specialties} and level-up existing ones. The experience you gain is multiplied by your {Nutrition} and {Housing|Housing Overview}.
trans-unit.target: 蓬勃发展的经济的核心是专业化和贸易的能力，因此与你尝试自己做的一切相比，工作效率更高。实现一个成功的社会取决于成功实现两者。公民的技能使他们拥有独特的能力和增强能力，这使他们能够与在经济中拥有不同技能的其他公民[Trade]。获得[Experience] 后，你就可以解锁新的[Specialties] 并升级现有的。你获得的经验将乘以[Nutrition] 和[Housing|Housing Overview]。

trans-unit.source: The skill system allows citizens to specialize in specific trades.
trans-unit.target: 技能体系使公民能够专注于特定行业。

trans-unit.source:
Specialties are unlocked as you level-up from Character {Experience}. Each specialty (represented with a star) will unlock new abilities you can use.
Professions and Specialties {Professions|Profession Overview} are simply groupings of skills, and don’t serve any other purpose besides storing similar skills together. Specialties are each contained under a profession and require two steps to gain.
1. First, you must read a skill scroll for the skill. 2. Next, you must spend a star to take the specialty.
Once you have chosen the specialty, you gain the base-level abilities associated with it. As you perform actions related to that specialty, you will gain Specialty {Experience}, leveling up the specialty and unlocking new abilities to it. You can see the spread of specialties that other citizens have chosen on the skill selection dialog, and it is often a good idea to choose specialties that other players have not chosen or leveled up. This will allow you to each perform your own specialties and {Trade} with each other for a net gain.
trans-unit.target: 当你从角色[Experience] 升级时，专业会解锁。每个专业（以星号表示）都会解锁你可以使用的新功能。专业和专业[Professions|Profession Overview] 只是技能的分组，除了将相似的技能存储在一起之外，没有其他目的。每个专业都包含在一个专业下，并且需要两个步骤才能获得。 1. 首先，你必须阅读技能卷轴。 2. 接下来，你必须花费一颗星星来攻读特色菜。选择专业后，你将获得与之相关的基本能力。当你执行与该专业相关的动作时，你将获得专业[Experience]，升级专业并为其解锁新功能。你可以在技能选择对话框中看到其他公民选择的专业的传播，选择其他玩家未选择或升级的专业通常是个好主意。这样一来，你就可以各自执行自己的专业，并彼此进行[Trade] 以获得净收益。

trans-unit.source: Specialties can be gained by collecting Character Experience, and each specialty will unlock new unique abilities.
trans-unit.target: 可以通过收集角色经验来获得专业，每个专业都将解锁新的独特能力。

trans-unit.source: Food, along with housing, is essential for increasing your skills.
trans-unit.target: 食物和住房对提高技能至关重要。

trans-unit.source:
Carpenters are specialist at working with wood.
- {LoggingSkill} allows citizens to chop trees faster while expending less calories. In addition, this specialty is used to produce {HewnLogItem} which are the primary building material for the carpentry profession. - {CarpentrySkill} allows citizens to produce wooden furniture and {Boards|Board Item}. By using a {SawmillItem}, citizens with this specialty can produce {LumberItem} and higher quality furniture. - {CompositesSkill} allows citizens to use advanced technology to produce the finest quality wood building material. - {PaperMillingSkill} allows citizens to refine wood pulp into {PaperItem}. Paper is used for some specialty furniture.
trans-unit.target: 木匠是处理木材的专家。 -[LoggingSkill] 可使公民更快地砍树，而消耗的卡路里更少。另外，此专业用于生产[HewnLogItem]，这是木工行业的主要建筑材料。 -[CarpentrySkill] 允许公民生产木制家具和[Boards|Board Item]。通过使用[SawmillItem]，具有此专业的公民可以生产[LumberItem] 和更高品质的家具。 -[CompositesSkill] 允许公民使用先进的技术来生产最优质的木建筑材料。 -[PaperMillingSkill] 允许公民将木浆提炼成[PaperItem]。纸用于一些特殊家具。

trans-unit.source: Carpenters are specialist at working with wood.
trans-unit.target: 木匠是处理木材的专家。

trans-unit.source:
Chefs cook high quality food that enables citizens to gain experience faster. Using the raw food produced by farmers and hunters, chefs create food that is high in nutrients. As technology advances, the chef specialties cook progressively higher quality food.
- {CampfireCookingSkill} is the entry level Chef specialty that uses a {CampfireItem} to prepare better meals than the basic charred food produced by non-chefs. - {CookingSkill} is the second step in the Chef profession that uses a {CastIronStoveItem} to produce higher quality meals. - {BakingSkill} is an alternative to Cooking that uses {FlourItem} to produce baked goods. - {AdvancedCookingSkill} is a high-level Chef profession that produces the highest tier of food. - {AdvancedBakingSkill} is an alternative to Advanced Cooking that uses {YeastItem} to produce high quality baked goods with {LeavenedDoughItem}.
trans-unit.target: 厨师烹饪高品质的食物，使公民能够更快地获得经验。厨师使用农民和猎人生产的生食，制作出营养丰富的食物。随着技术的进步，专业厨师逐渐烹饪更高品质的食物。 -[CampfireCookingSkill] 是入门级厨师专业，它使用[CampfireItem] 来准备比非厨师生产的基本烧焦的饭菜更好的饭菜。 -[CookingSkill] 是厨师职业的第二步，它使用[CastIronStoveItem] 来制作更高质量的饭菜。 -[BakingSkill] 是烹饪的替代方法，它使用[FlourItem] 生产烘焙食品。 -[AdvancedCookingSkill] 是一种高级厨师职业，可生产最高等级的食物。 -[AdvancedBakingSkill] 是高级烹饪的替代方法，它使用[YeastItem] 与[LeavenedDoughItem] 生产高质量的烘焙食品。

trans-unit.source: Chefs cook high quality food that enables citizens to gain experience faster.
trans-unit.target: 厨师烹饪高品质的食物，使公民能够更快地获得经验。

trans-unit.source:
Engineers use the materials created by other professions to lead the way in technological advancement. The vehicles they produce greatly improve the capacity for trade and the speed at which citizens work. The crafting tables they produce are used by a variety of professions to create high-end goods. Being an engineer requires frequent interaction with other professions as engineers are unable to produce the primary inputs necessary for crafting.
-{BasicEngineeringSkill} allows citizens to produce roads and carts to help with transportation. In addition, basic engineering creates the crafting tables necessary to process and smelt ore. -{MechanicsSkill} allows to citizens to create early motorized vehicles, like a {SteamTruckItem}. Mechanics provides the first source of electric power to help enable tecnological progression. -{IndustrySkill} allows citizens to create advanced vehicles, like an {ExcavatorItem}, to quickly collect large amounts of natural resources. -{OilDrillingSkill} allows citizens to use a {PumpJackItem} to collect {PetroleumItem} for use in fuel and a variety of industrial projects. -{ElectronicsSkill} is a high-end engineer specialty that uses expensive ingredients to produce the most advanced materials needed for modern crafting projects. In addition to this and providing a source of green electricity, electronics is used to craft the {LaserItem} needed to save the world from the meteor.
trans-unit.target: 工程师使用其他行业创造的材料来引领技术进步。他们生产的车辆大大提高了贸易能力和公民的工作速度。他们生产的手工桌被各种专业人士用来制造高端产品。成为工程师需要与其他专业人士频繁互动，因为工程师无法提供制作所需的主要投入。 -[BasicEngineeringSkill] 允许公民生产道路和手推车以帮助运输。此外，基础工程还创建了处理和冶炼矿石所需的工艺表。 -[MechanicsSkill] 允许公民创建早期的机动车辆，例如[SteamTruckItem]。力学提供了第一个电力来源，以帮助实现技术发展。 -[IndustrySkill] 允许公民创建先进的工具，例如[ExcavatorItem]，以快速收集大量自然资源。 -[OilDrillingSkill] 允许公民使用[PumpJackItem] 收集[PetroleumItem] 以用于燃料和各种工业项目。 -[ElectronicsSkill] 是高端工程师专业，使用昂贵的原料生产现代工艺项目所需的最先进的材料。除此之外，它还提供了绿色电力，电子产品被用来制作[LaserItem] 来拯救流星世界。

trans-unit.source: Engineers are master inventors who produce vehicles and other advanced products for other citizens.
trans-unit.target: 工程师是为其他公民生产车辆和其他先进产品的主要发明家。

trans-unit.source:
Farmers are specialists in agriculture. The crops they produce are a primary source of the calories needed for citizens to perform work.
- {FarmingSkill} allows citizens to create seeds out of produce to help create large farms. - {FertilizersSkill} allows citizens add nutrients to soil and test soil conditions with a {SoilSamplerItem}. - {GatheringSkill} allows citizens to harvest more produce from both wild plants and human-created farms. - {MillingSkill} allows citizens to process produce in a {MillItem} for use by chefs in more advanced recipes.
trans-unit.target: 农民是农业专家。他们生产的农作物是公民从事工作所需的热量的主要来源。 -[FarmingSkill] 允许公民使用农产品生产种子来帮助建立大型农场。 -[FertilizersSkill] 允许公民使用[SoilSamplerItem] 向土壤添加营养并测试土壤条件。 -[GatheringSkill] 使公民可以从野生植物和人工农场中收获更多农产品。 -[MillingSkill] 允许公民在[MillItem] 中加工产品，以供厨师使用更高级的配方。

trans-unit.source: Farmers are specialists in agriculture.
trans-unit.target: 农民是农业专家。

trans-unit.source:
Hunters are specialists at efficiently hunting animals and preparing meat for use by chefs. The two hunter specialties are closely linked as the animals hunted must be processed before it can be used by a chef.
- {ButcherySkill} allows citizens to process animal carcasses into prepared meat for chefs. The pelts and hides they create can be used for clothing and other crafting projects. - {HuntingSkill} grants citizens bonus damage when hunting animals and allows them to approach animals at a closer distance without causing them to run.
trans-unit.target: 猎人是有效狩猎动物和准备肉类供厨师使用的专家。这两个猎人专业紧密相关，因为被狩猎的动物必须经过加工才能被厨师使用。 -[ButcherySkill] 允许公民将动物遗骸加工成厨师准备的肉。他们制造的兽皮和皮革可以用于服装和其他手工项目。 -[HuntingSkill] 在狩猎动物时给予公民额外的伤害，并使他们能够近距离接近动物而不会使其奔跑。

trans-unit.source: Hunters are specialists at hunting animals and preparing meat for chefs.
trans-unit.target: 猎人是狩猎动物和为厨师准备肉类的专家。

trans-unit.source:
Masons are specialists at mining and working with stone. They provide a valuable source of building material and furniture for homes. The ore they collect is used by Smiths to smelt metal bars.
-{MiningSkill} allows citizens to expend less calories while mining stone and ore. Miners also process the collected ore and concentrate it to be used for smelting. -{MasonrySkill} allows citizens to produce {MortarItem}, a primary ingredient for most mason building materials. The {MortaredStoneItem} they produce is a great source of early housing material. -{PotterySkill} allows citizens to craft {BrickItem} building material and furniture out of {ClayItem}. -{GlassworkingSkill} allows citizens to create {GlassItem} and later {FramedGlassItem}. Glass is a nice supplemental buildling material and is used in a variety of industrial projects. -{AdvancedMasonrySkill} is a high-level mason skill that produces the finest quality Ashlar Stone building material and furniture.
trans-unit.target: 石匠是采矿和处理石材的专家。它们为房屋提供了宝贵的建筑材料和家具资源。 工匠们使用他们收集的矿石来冶炼钢筋。 -[MiningSkill] 可让公民在开采石头和矿石时消耗更少的卡路里。矿工还对收集的矿石进行处理并将其浓缩以用于冶炼。 -[MasonrySkill] 使公民可以生产[MortarItem]，这是大多数泥瓦匠建筑材料的主要成分。他们生产的[MortaredStoneItem] 是早期房屋材料的重要来源。 -[PotterySkill] 允许公民从[ClayItem] 中制作[BrickItem] 建筑材料和家具。 -[GlassworkingSkill] 允许公民创建[GlassItem]，然后再创建[FramedGlassItem]。玻璃是一种很好的补充建筑材料，可用于各种工业项目。 -[AdvancedMasonrySkill] 是一种高级泥工技能，可生产最优质的Ashlar Stone建筑材料和家具。

trans-unit.source: Masons are specialist at mining and working with stone.
trans-unit.target: 石匠是采矿和石材加工方面的专家。

trans-unit.source:
Professions are groups of similar {Specialties} that unlock new crafting recipes and provide abilities like reduced calorie usage when crafting or using a tool. Within each profession is a group of similar specialties.
Below is a list of all professions and the corresponding specialties. Citizens can choose to focus on one profession or learn specialties from a variety of professions.
trans-unit.target: 专业是一组类似的[Specialties]，可以解锁新的制作配方，并提供诸如在制作或使用工具时减少卡路里消耗等功能。每个专业中都有一组相似的专业。以下是所有专业和相应专业的列表。公民可以选择专注于一个专业，也可以从各种专业中学习专业

trans-unit.source: Professions grant citizens special abilities and allow them to craft advanced items.
trans-unit.target: 专业赋予公民特殊的能力和制造高级物品的能力。

trans-unit.source:
Smiths smelt concentrated ore into metal bars that can be used by a variety of professions. In addition, Smiths create superior tools that help citizens work more productively.
- {SmeltingSkill} are specialists at making the metal bars that make up the foundation of technological advancement. The iron tools they create provide significant benefit over the early stone tools. - {AdvancedSmeltingSkill} allows citizens to further refine iron bars into {Steel|SteelBarItem} for use in industry and high-tier building materials. Citizens with this specialty can use the steel they create to even more modern tools than the iron tools created by basic smelting.
trans-unit.target: 工匠们将浓缩的矿石冶炼成金属条，各种专业人士都可以使用。此外，工匠创建了出色的工具，可帮助公民提高工作效率。 -[SmeltingSkill] 是制造组件技术进步基础的钢筋的专家。他们制造的铁工具比早期的石材工具具有明显的优势。 -[AdvancedSmeltingSkill] 允许公民进一步将铁锭精炼成[Steel|SteelBarItem]，以用于工业和高层建筑材料。具有这种专业的公民可以将他们制造的钢用于比通过基本冶炼产生的铁工具更现代的工具。

trans-unit.source: Smiths smelt ore into metal bars and create advanced tools.
trans-unit.target: 工匠们将矿石熔炼成钢筋并创建高级工具。

trans-unit.source:
All citizens are survialists in the world of Eco.
Experience in {SelfImprovementSkill} is gained anytime a citizen levels up another specialty. Self-improvement has two main benefits. As citizens progress, they will have increased carry weight capacity and a larger stomach capcity for extra calories. In addition, less calories are consumed when using hammers, shovels, and drills.
trans-unit.target: 在《生态物语》世界中，所有公民都是生存主义者。每当公民升级另一个专业时，就可以获得[SelfImprovementSkill] 方面的经验。自我完善有两个主要好处。随着公民的进步，他们将具有更大的承载能力和更大的胃内吸收更多卡路里的能力。此外，使用锤子，铁锹和钻头消耗的卡路里更少。

trans-unit.source: Survialists expend less calories when using basic tools and have increased carrying weight.
trans-unit.target: 生存主义者在使用基本工具时消耗的卡路里更少，并且携带的体重增加了。

trans-unit.source:
Tailors work with cloth to make clothing and high-end furniture for other citizens. Unlike other professions, the Tailor profession has only one specialty.
{TailoringSkill} allows citizens to create {ClothItem} for use in crafting projects by a variety of professions. The clothing tailors creates provides citizens with special bonuses. For example, a {BigBackpackItem} has a higher maximum weight limit than the starter {BasicBackpackItem}.
trans-unit.target: 裁缝用布做衣服，为其他公民制作衣服和高档家具。与其他专业不同，裁缝专业只有一个专业。 [TailoringSkill] 允许公民创建[ClothItem]，以供各种专业人士制作项目。裁缝制作的服装为公民提供了特殊的奖励。例如，[BigBackpackItem] 的最大重量限制比起步器[BasicBackpackItem] 的最大重量限制高。

trans-unit.source: Tailors work with cloth to make clothing and high-end furniture for other citizens.
trans-unit.target: 裁缝用布为其他公民制作衣服和高档家具。

trans-unit.source:
Any object containing a Vehicle Component can be mounted and interacted with in a variety of ways, providing a number of benefits.
Many vehicles require {Power|Power Component} to operate and each have their own {Storage|Storage Component}. Building infrastructure around the world will greatly enhance your {Transport} capabilites as many vehicles require roads to operate.
trans-unit.target: 任何包含车辆部件的物体都可以通过多种方式进行安装和交互，从而带来许多好处。许多车辆需要[Power|Power Component] 才能运行，并且每个车辆都有自己的[Storage|Storage Component]。世界各地的基础设施建设将大大提高你的[Transport] 功能，因为许多车辆都需要道路来运营。

trans-unit.source:
- Transportation: Vehicles like the {WoodCartItem} can transport goods and people from place to place. As technology progresses, more advanced vehicles become available with faster movement speeds and higher carrying capacity at the cost of creating pollution.
- Excavation: The {ExcavatorItem} and {SkidSteerItem} can quickly move large quantites of dirt and rock. This will greatly increase the speed of {Mining} and allow areas of land to be quickly cleared for new construction projects.
- Contruction: The {CraneItem} can quickly perform large-scale {Building} projects. The crane has a much larger building range than a citizen who is building with a hammer and can access large amounts of storage.
trans-unit.target: -运输：[WoodCartItem] 之类的车辆可以从一个地方到另一个地方运输货物和人员。随着技术的进步，更先进的车辆将以更快的移动速度和更高的承载能力出现，而以造成污染为代价。-挖掘：[ExcavatorItem] 和[SkidSteerItem] 能够快速移动大量的泥土和岩石。这将大大提高[Mining] 的速度，并允许快速清理土地以用于新的建设项目。 -结构：[CraneItem] 可以快速执行大型[Building] 项目。该起重机的建筑范围比用锤子建造并可以使用大量存储空间的居民大得多。

trans-unit.source: Vehicles can be used for performing a variety of tasks.
trans-unit.target: 车辆可用于执行各种任务。

trans-unit.source:
In Eco, players harvest food initially from the wild biomes around them. Plants ripe for the opportunity to harvest sustainably abound. Wild food sources can serve as a supplementary renewable resource, but will quickly crash if taken advantage of. Some plants will be useful for more than just food-- they may provide chemical compounds that can be found no-where else, or the raw materials needed for textiles and clothing.
Different plants require different tools to harvest, such as a shovel, or a scythe. Other species can be picked by hand without a tool. Similarly, some plants are killed when they are harvested, and others will simply re-grow the picked fruits if they continue to grow and be healthy.
trans-unit.target: 在《生态物语》中，玩家最初是从周围的野生生物群落中收获食物的。成熟的植物有机会可持续地收获。野生食物资源可以作为补充的可再生资源，但如果利用的话，它将迅速崩溃。有些植物不仅可以用于食物，还可以提供其他地方无法找到的化学化合物，或纺织和服装所需的原材料。不同的植物需要不同的收获工具，例如铲子或镰刀。无需工具即可手动采摘其他物种。同样，有些植物在收获时会被摧毁，而其他一些如果继续生长并保持健康就只会重新种植采摘的果实。

trans-unit.source: The living system of plants and animals which provides the means for humans to survive.
trans-unit.target: 动植物的生存系统为人类的生存提供了手段。

trans-unit.source:
Animal species are inhabitants of the world in Eco that vary greatly in terms of possible use as resources. Some species make for excellent sources of meat and food like milk, where others may provide useful byproducts like wool, leather, tallow, or bone meal. But animals also vary greatly in terms of their resilience and adaptability to impacts on their populations, habitat, and food sources.
Through hunting, players can use various traps and hand tools to harvest wild animals directly from the ecosystems they live in for use. Hunting can also serve as a way to control or cull excessive populations of animals. Hunting will be an important but not strictly necessary activity for players especially earlier on in order to meet the appetite of a large society.
Hunting an animal is only the first step in making the most use out of it. Butchery advancements will allow players to process and use animals for a large variety of animal products. Collaboration with other professions like cooks and tailors will unlock many nutritional, industrial, and even cultural uses for animals and their products, not all of which need be obtained through hunting. However, if a particular animal product becomes popular in the economy, care will have to be taken to avoid destructive pressure on a species. Hunting is often the final blow to an endangered species whose products become more and more valuable as they become more and more rare.
To get started with hunting, you'll need to craft a {WoodenBowItem} and some {Arrows|Arrow Item}. More advanced bows will make it easier to hit your target quickly. Keep in mind that animals can be killed more quickly with a well-aimed shot, and that it may be easier to hunt some animals based on the time of day.
trans-unit.target: 动物物种是《生态物语》世界中的居民，在作为资源的用途方面差异很大。有些物种是牛奶和肉类食物的极好来源，而另一些物种则可能提供有用的副产品，例如羊毛，皮革，牛脂或骨粉。但是动物在适应能力，种族群落，生存环境和食物来源的影响方面也差异很大。通过狩猎，玩家可以使用各种陷阱和手动工具直接从其生活使用的生态系统中收获野生动物。狩猎还可以作为控制或剔除过多动物种群的一种方式。为了满足广大社会的胃口，狩猎对于玩家来说是一项重要但并非绝对必要的活动，尤其是在早期。狩猎动物只是充分利用动物的第一步。肉加工业的发展将使玩家能够加工和使用用于多种动物产品的动物。与厨师和裁缝师等其他专业人士的合作将解锁动物及其产品的许多营养，工业甚至文化用途，而并非所有这些都需要通过狩猎获得。但是，如果特定的动物产品在经济中变得流行，则必须小心避免对物种造成破坏性压力。狩猎往往是对濒临灭绝物种的最后一击，随着它们变得越来越稀有，其产品变得越来越有价值。要开始狩猎，你需要制作一个[WoodenBowItem] 和一些[Arrows|Arrow Item]。更高级的弓箭将使你更容易快速击中目标。请记住，使用有针对性的射击可以更快地摧毁动物，并且根据一天中的时间来狩猎某些动物可能更容易。

trans-unit.source: The living system of plants and animals which provides the means for humans to survive.
trans-unit.target: 动植物的生存系统为人类的生存提供了手段。

trans-unit.source: Ecopedia guide for getting started.
trans-unit.target: 《生态物语》百科入门指南。

trans-unit.source: Once you've covered your basic needs, it's time to think about how you want to contribute to the joint project of society, and your profession is how you do that.
trans-unit.target: 满足基本需求之后，就该考虑如何为社会共同计划做出贡献了，而你的专业就是你如何做到这一点的。

trans-unit.source: Each profession has a number of {Specialties} beneath it which will unlock special abilities. Because citizens can only have a limited number of specialties, a good approach is to take different specialties and then {Trade} between each other. In the skills window, you can see how many citizens have chosen each profession and look for the most-needed skills, choosing those to focus on.
trans-unit.target: 每个职业下面都有许多[Specialties]，它们可以释放特殊能力。因为公民只能拥有数量有限的专业，所以一个好的方法是先选择不同的专业，然后在彼此之间进行[Trade]。在技能窗口中，你可以查看有多少公民选择了每种职业，并寻找最需要的技能，然后选择要关注的技能。

trans-unit.source: Check out the {Profession Overview} for a tour of the different options.
trans-unit.target: 查看[Profession Overview]，以了解不同选项。

trans-unit.source: (Note: the skills dialog is hidden until later in the tutorial. It can be made visible by completing the tutorial or skipping it.)
trans-unit.target: （注意：技能对话框在本教程的后面将一直隐藏。可以通过完成本教程或跳过它来使其可见。）

trans-unit.source: A world with a range of professions can trade for mutual benefits.
trans-unit.target: 一个拥有多种专业的世界可以互惠互利。

trans-unit.source:
The {Government|Government Overview} of Eco is run by individual citizens, and the quality of governance is fully dependent on how those citizens participate.
There are a number of ways to participate in the running of the government, including:
- Founding the government by creating a {CapitolItem} and {Constitution}. - Defining civics objects including {Districts}, {Demographics}, {Elected Titles}, {Election Processes}, and {Amendments|Constitution}. - Running as a candidate for an {Elected Title|Elected Titles}, giving special powers assigned to it by the constitution. - Enacting {Laws} after attaining the privilege to (defined in the constitution). - Enacting {Executive Actions}, with privileges also defined in the constitution. - Voting in {Elections} for {Elected Titles} and other civic actions. - Debating laws with the use of {Data}, presenting arguments to your fellow citizens arguing for one choice over another.
trans-unit.target: 《生态物语》的[Government|Government Overview] 由个人公民管理，治理的质量完全取决于这些公民的参与方式。参与公会运行的方式有很多，包括：-通过创建[CapitolItem] 和[Constitution] 成立公会。 -定义公民对象，包括[Districts]，[Demographics]，[Elected Titles]，[Election Processes] 和[Amendments|Constitution]。 -推选[Elected Title|Elected Titles] 候选人，赋予公约赋予其特殊权力。 -获得（在公约中定义的）特权后制定[Laws]。 -制定[Executive Actions]，并在公约中也定义特权。 -在[Elections] 中投票推选“[Elected Titles]”和其他公民行为。 -使用[Data] 辩论章程，向你的同胞提出争论，要求他们选择另一种选择。

trans-unit.source: Each of these options are available at a variety of locations which must be constructed by citizens. The current government can be viewed by selecting the Government icon in the action bar and hovering over each section will specify where the civic object is defined.
trans-unit.target: 这些选项中的每一个都可以在必须由公民建造的多个位置使用。可以通过在操作栏中选择“公会”图标来查看当前公会，将鼠标悬停在每个部分上将指定定义公民对象的位置。

trans-unit.source: Your civic duties are the many different ways you can interact with the government.
trans-unit.target: 你的公民职责是你与公会互动的许多不同方式。

trans-unit.source:
To bring a society through the ages to the peaks of technology, a thriving {Economy|Economy Overview} is needed, and by participating in it, citizens can both help progress society as well advance their own abilities and wealth.
Participating in the economy takes place through {Trade} of both goods and services. Finding existing settlements with {Stores|Store Component}, {Contracts}, and {Work Parties} will give citizens the possibility to trade the goods and labor they specialize in for the specialties of others, achieving their individual goals faster than if they had each worked alone. As you take on new projects that require materials or labor that you lack the ability to create or perform on your own, you can instead, sell goods and offer services on the market by creating stores and setting prices in a {Currency} of your choice. Using this currency to purchase the goods and services of others will then allow you to reach those goals without having to learn everything yourself.
As you build up your capabilities and have more needs of other citizens, you can create your own stores, contracts, and work parties to seek their aid. As you acquire wealth, your power to effect change (both good and bad) rises.
trans-unit.target: 为了使一个社会不断发展达到技术的巅峰，需要蓬勃发展的[Economy|Economy Overview]，通过参与其中，公民既可以帮助社会进步，也可以提高自己的能力和财富。通过商品和服务的[Trade] 参与经济。通过[Stores|Store Component]，[Contracts] 和[Work Parties] 查找现有的定居点，将使公民能够将自己擅长的商品和劳力交易为其他人的专业，从而比单独工作更快地实现其个人目标。 在进行需要材料或人工的新项目时，你自己没有创造或执行的能力，而是可以通过在[Currency] 中创建商店和设置价格来出售可以在市场上执行的商品和服务。你的选择。然后，使用这种货币购买他人的商品和服务将使你可以实现这些目标，而不必自己学习所有东西。当你建立自己的能力并满足其他公民的更多需求时，你可以创建自己的商店，合同和工作团队以寻求他们的帮助。当你获得财富时，影响改变（好的和坏的）的能力就会增强。

trans-unit.source: The most efficient way to progress in knowledge and technology is trade within the economy.
trans-unit.target: 知识和技术进步的最有效方法是经济体内的贸易。

trans-unit.source: While a functioning society is necessary to stop the meteor, each individual must have their own needs met, starting with food and shelter.
trans-unit.target: 虽然必须有一个运转良好的社会来阻止流星，但每个人都必须满足自己的需求，从食物和庇护开始。

trans-unit.source: Choosing a Settlement
trans-unit.target: 选择定居点

trans-unit.source: When choosing a location to settle in, you'll want to consider the following factors:
trans-unit.target: 在选择居住地点时，你需要考虑以下因素:

trans-unit.source:
Proximity to Resources Natural food sources and building supplies nearby will greatly speed up initial progress.
Proximity to Others Setting up near other settlements offers a distinct advantage, as you can then trade with others to gain progress faster. However, an overcrowded area may also have limited options for expansion and natural food in the vicinity. You'll want to find a balance based on personal preference.
trans-unit.target:
靠近资源 天然的食物来源和附近的建筑用品将大大加快初步进度。
与他人的距离 接近在其他居民点附近建房具有明显的优势，因为你可以与其他人交易以更快地取得进展。但是，人满为患的地区在附近扩展和天然食品方面的选择也可能有限。你将根据个人喜好找到一个平衡点。

trans-unit.source: Claiming Your Plot
trans-unit.target: 认领你的地块

trans-unit.source: Once you've selected a place to build your first settlement, you'll want to claim {Property} around it. When placing your starting tent this will automatically occur, and further property can be claimed with land papers. Property you claim will be protected from other citizens, unless you give them access.
trans-unit.target: 选择要建立第一个住所的地点后，你将需要在其附近认领[Property]。当放置你的起始帐篷时，这将自动发生，并且可以使用契据进一步认领土地。你认领的土地将受到其他公民的保护，除非你允许他们访问。

trans-unit.source: Food
trans-unit.target: 食物

trans-unit.source: Finding a food supply is critical to having the calories you need to work. Additionally, the {Nutrition} of the food you eat impacts how fast you gain experience, so you'll want to eat the best food available for the fastest gains. When settling in the wilderness, look for food sources nearby you can take from, or plan to setup a {Farm|Farming}. When settling near an existing village, have a look at the local economy to see what you can {Trade} in order to get food.
trans-unit.target: 寻找食物供应对于拥有工作所需的卡路里至关重要。此外，你所吃食物的[Nutrition] 会影响你获得经验的速度，因此，你将想吃最好的食物以获得最快的收获。在野外定居时，请在附近寻找食物来源，或计划建立[Farm|Farming]。在现有村庄附近定居时，请看看当地的经济状况，看看你可以[Trade] 以获取食物。

trans-unit.source: Setting up a source of food and shelter is a first priority.
trans-unit.target: 建立食物和住房的来源是第一要务。

trans-unit.source:
The {Ecosystem|Ecosystem Overview} is the provider of a vast number of resources citizens can make use of, but it is also vulnerable to their impact. Succeeding as a society will require intelligent management of impact, using the {Government|Government Overview} to regulate what is allowed.
Various forms of {Pollution} can affect the environment, creating the potential for catastrophes that could destroy your food supply and halt progress, leading to ecological collapse. Using {Data} tools, citizens can monitor the impact they're having on the world and use that data to argue for specific regulations to protect it, or accelerate the economy in other avenues.
The world begins as a vast untouched wilderness, and through the ages citizens will need to radically transform it using technology to build the industry and knowledge required to stop {The Meteor}. Citizens will need to execute a careful navigation between using the resources of the ecosystem maximally and avoid damaging the environment beyond repair.
trans-unit.target: [Ecosystem|Ecosystem Overview] 是公民可以使用的大量资源提供者，但也容易受到其影响。要成为一个成功的社会，就需要对影响进行智能管理，使用[Government|Government Overview] 来规范允许的范围。各种形式的[Pollution] 都可能影响环境，造成潜在的灾难，这些灾难可能破坏你的食物供应并阻碍进度，导致生态崩溃。公民可以使用[Data] 工具来监控自己对世界的影响，并使用这些数据来主张制定特定的法规来保护它，或者以其他方式促进经济发展。世界始于广阔无人的荒野，随着时代的发展，公民将需要使用技术从根本上改变它，以建立制止[The Meteor] 所需的产业和知识。公民将需要在最大程度地利用生态系统资源与避免对生态环境造成无法修复的影响之间进行仔细的导航。

trans-unit.source: The ecosystem provides many resources, but is vulnerable to human actions.
trans-unit.target: 生态系统提供了许多资源，但易受人类行为的影响。

trans-unit.source:
Eco is a world bookended by two threats: Environmental collapse, and the ever-looming {Meteor|The Meteor}.
To succeed, you'll need to progress through the ages, advancing science and industry to a level that can destroy the meteor without causing ecological collapse in the process. You will do this as an individual member of a larger society, and the creation of a positive interplay between individual needs and societal needs is critical to success.
Your focus in Eco will be on managing your individual progress while simultaneously working to progress the {Economy|Economy Overview} as a whole and doing so inside a rich {Ecosystem|Ecosystem Overview} of plants and animals that are affected by what you do.
To aid in creating a positive relationship between this triangle of Individuals, Economy, and Ecosystem, you have the tools of {Government|Government Overview} at your disposal. You can set binding {Laws} that determine what is allowed and run for {Election|Elections} to gain governmental powers to make changes and guide society.
But first matters first, you'll need to take care of your basic needs before you can save the world. To do that, you'll start with {Food and Shelter}.
trans-unit.target: 《生态物语》世界充满了两个威胁：环境崩溃和迫在眉睫的[Meteor|The Meteor]。要取得成功，你需要不断发展，将科学和工业发展到可以摧毁流星而又不会在此过程中造成生态崩溃的水平。你将作为一个更大的社会中的个体来做到这一点，并且在个体需求和社会需求之间建立积极的相互作用对于成功至关重要。你在生态方面的重点将是在管理自己的进步的同时，同时整体上推进[Economy|Economy Overview]，并在受你的工作影响的丰富的[Ecosystem|Ecosystem Overview] 植物和动物中进行这项工作。为了帮助在个人，经济和生态系统的这个三角形之间建立积极的关系，你可以使用[Government|Government Overview] 工具。你可以设置约束[Laws] 来确定允许的内容并进行[Election|Elections] 推选，以获取公会进行变革和指导社会的权力。但是首先要考虑的是，你需要先满足基本需求，然后才能拯救世界。为此，你将从[Food and Shelter] 开始。

trans-unit.source: Your journey in Eco.
trans-unit.target: 你的《生态物语》之旅。

trans-unit.source:
The world has been given an expiration date, a meteor in a collapsing orbit around the globe, set to collide in 30 real-time days, destroying everything on it in the process.
However, hope is not lost, for with the right technology this crisis can be prevented. By working together to generate a thriving {Economy|economy overview} and advance progress with {Research}, humanity has a chance to build the {Laser|LaserItem} needed to destroy this threat.
It will not be an easy task, and the industry required to reach such levels of advancement will pose a real threat to the {Ecosystem|Ecosystem Overview}. To combat this and avoid falling victim to an ecological collapse of your own creation, a well-regulated {Government|Government Overview} must be designed, implemented, and created by its citizens.
The fate of the world depends on its citizens acting together in harmony with the ecosystem to achieve great technological feats, and only through studied collaboration can they succeed.
trans-unit.target: 地球已经定下了失效日期，这是一颗流星正在地球坍缩中，将在30天实时碰撞，在此过程中摧毁一切。但是，希望并没有失去，因为使用正确的技术可以避免这种危机。通过共同努力，发展出蓬勃发展的[Economy|economy overview] 并推动[Research] 的发展，人类有机会建立消灭这种威胁所需的[Laser|LaserItem]。这将不是一件容易的事，要达到这种进步水平的行业将对[Ecosystem|Ecosystem Overview] 构成真正的威胁。为了避免这种情况，并避免成为你自己的创造物生态崩溃的受害者，必须由其公民设计，实施和创造受严格监管的[Government|Government Overview]。世界的命运取决于其公民与生态系统和谐合作以实现巨大的技术成就，只有通过研究合作，他们才能成功。

trans-unit.source: A looming threat from above.
trans-unit.target: 迫在眉睫的威胁。

trans-unit.source: Through the use of tools, citizens can build great structures and move mountains. The starting tools will allow you to effect small change only, but as technology advances you will gain tools that can perform batch operations, and eventually {Vehicle|Vehicle Component} that can make massive transformations to the world.
trans-unit.target: 通过使用工具，公民可以建造高大的建筑物并移动山脉。入门工具将只允许你进行小改动，但是随着技术的进步，你将获得可以执行批处理操作的工具，最终可以使用[Vehicle|Vehicle Component] 进行大规模的改造。

trans-unit.source:
There are a number of different tool types available: - {Building} Tools. Hammers will allow you to place construction materials in 'Forms', shovels allow moving soil. - {Farming} Tools. Plowing, planting, and sowing all have tools to increase their efficiency. - {Transport} Tools. Vehicles and tools for constructing roads are especially useful for transporting goods. - {Harvesting} Tools. These tools allow collection of resources from the ecosystem. - {Hunting} Tools. Hunting animals for food requires specialized tools.
Tools that are manually powered require Calories, affecting your {Nutrition}. All tools have Durability associated with them, which drops as they are used. Once a tool has lost its durability, it will take a tremendous amount of calories to use, and should be repaired at a {Repair|Repair Component} Table.
trans-unit.target: 有许多不同的工具类型可用：-[Building] 工具。锤子将允许你将建筑材料放入“表格”中，而铁铲则可以使土壤移动。 -[Farming] 工具。耕作，播种和播种都有提高其效率的工具。 -[Transport] 工具。修路的车辆和工具对运输货物特别有用。 -[Harvesting] 工具。这些工具允许从生态系统中收集资源。 -[Hunting] 工具。猎食动物需要专门的工具。手动供电的工具需要卡路里，从而影响你的[Nutrition]。所有工具都具有与之关联的耐用性，使用时会下降。工具失去耐用性后，将需要消耗大量卡路里，应在[Repair|Repair Component] 表中进行维修。

trans-unit.source: Having access to the best tools allow you to achieve the most progress.
trans-unit.target: 使用最好的工具可以使你取得最大的进步。

trans-unit.source: After collecting some basic raw materials, building is one of the first things a citizen will do to establish themselves in the world.
trans-unit.target: 在收集了一些基本原材料之后，建筑是公民在世界上建立自己的第一步。

trans-unit.source: Using a hammer or a crane, citizens can create buildings for homes and industry.
trans-unit.target: 公民可以使用锤子或起重机来建造房屋和工业建筑物。

trans-unit.source: The My Deeds Component allows citizens to view and manage all {Property} that they own, providing an interface to access them easily.
trans-unit.target: 通过“我的行为”组件，公民可以查看和管理他们拥有的所有[Property]，并提供了一个易于访问他们的界面。

trans-unit.source: Allows citizens to see view and manage all property they own.
trans-unit.target: 允许公民查看和管理他们拥有的所有土地。

trans-unit.source:
The Text Component can be found on Signs, {Vehicles|Vehicle Component}, and the {Distribution Station|Item Distribution Component}.
Text can be useful for giving directions, advertising, or just writing a friendly greeting.
For those familar with text markup, a variety of tags may be used to alter the size, color, and formatting of text.
trans-unit.target: 文字组件可以在标牌，[Vehicles|Vehicle Component] 和[Distribution Station|Item Distribution Component] 上找到。文本可用于提供指示，广告或仅写友好的问候。对于那些熟悉文本标记的人，可以使用各种标签来更改文本的大小，颜色和格式。

trans-unit.source: Allows citizens write text that is displayed to other citizens.
trans-unit.target: 允许公民编写显示给其他公民的文字。

trans-unit.source:
A foundational goal of the ecosystem simulation in Eco is to contain a multitude of food webs. Food webs can be thought of as a set of 'consumer' and 'consumed' relationships between plants and animals that centers on a particular community of animals and their favorite foods. In general, the idea is to have at least one strong food web present in each biome. Food webs often are the basis of the most important inter and intra-relationships between species in an ecosystem. Many species rely on other species for food, or to hunt the species that is eating them.
Animal's prey and predators can be viewed through the web interface and checking the linked layers. Currently the prey-predator relationship is one way: the predators require the prey to exist, but they don't actually remove any from the simulation. This means that an explosion of elk would increase the wolf population, but this wouldn't put pressure onto the elk. Changing this relationship to consume both ways while ensuring it doesn't spiral out of control in an unexpected and sudden way is important as we move forward.
trans-unit.target: 《生态物语》中生态系统模拟的基本目标是包含大量食物网。食物网可以被认为是植物和动物之间的一组“消费”和“消费”关系，它们以特定的动物群落及其喜爱的食物为中心。通常，此想法是在每个生物群落中至少存在一个牢固的食物网。食物网通常是生态系统中物种之间最重要的内部和内部关系的基础。许多物种以其他物种为食，或捕食正在食用它们的物种。可以通过网络界面查看动物的猎物和掠食者，并检查链接的图层。当前，食肉动物与食肉动物之间的关系是一种方式：食肉动物需要食肉动物存在，但是它们实际上并没有从模拟中删除任何食肉动物。这意味着麋鹿的爆炸会增加狼的数量，但这不会给麋鹿带来压力。随着我们的前进，改变这种关系以同时使用两种方式，同时确保其不会以意外和突然的方式失控，这一点很重要。

trans-unit.source: The living system of plants and animals which provides the means for humans to survive.
trans-unit.target: 动植物的生存系统为人类的生存提供了手段。

trans-unit.source: Follow the development of Eco.
trans-unit.target: 跟随《生态物语》的发展。

trans-unit.source:
We intend Eco to be a forever-game, growing in depth and breadth and connections to other future games, a system of unlimited possibilities that aims to go beyond entertainment. This tree contains the past, present, and future of Eco.
Via our website, you can view all the interactive parts of the tree and see where we're headed and the guiding philosophy behind the game.
trans-unit.target: 我们希望《生态物语》能够成为一款永恒的游戏，其深度和广度将不断发展，并与其他未来游戏保持联系，这是一种超越娱乐无限可能性的系统。这棵树包含了《生态物语》的过去，现在和未来。通过我们的网站，你可以查看树的所有交互部分，了解我们前进的方向以及游戏背后的指导思想。

trans-unit.source: View Eco Tree at play.eco
trans-unit.target: 在查看生态树

trans-unit.source: Follow the development of Eco.
trans-unit.target: 跟随《生态物语》的发展。

trans-unit.source: Setting up a Store
trans-unit.target: 开设商店

trans-unit.source: Repairing Tools
trans-unit.target: 维修工具

trans-unit.source: Buildings in Eco
trans-unit.target: 《生态物语》建筑

trans-unit.source:
Buildings are divided up into {Rooms} and serve a variety of purposes.
Some of these uses include:
- Making a home filled with furniture to gain bonus {Experience} from housing
- Providing a space for {Crafting} stations to function
- Housing the various {Civic Objects|Civic Object Component} neccesary to establish a government
trans-unit.target: 建筑物分为[Rooms]，可用于多种用途。这些用途包括：-装满家具的房屋，以从住房中获得红利[Experience]-为[Crafting] 站提供运行空间-容纳各种[Civic Objects|Civic Object Component] 所需的建立公会。

trans-unit.source: Creating Buildings
trans-unit.target: 建立建筑物

trans-unit.source:
When carrying a building material, the hammer is able to switch between different block shapes and building patterns.
Citizens build their first homes using a {StoneHammerItem}, but as technology progresses increasingly better building tools become available that consume less calories when used.
Large-scale construction projects can be quickly completed with the use of a {CraneItem}, but at the cost of creating {Pollution}.
trans-unit.target: 当携带建筑材料时，锤子能够在不同的块形状和建筑样式之间切换。公民使用[StoneHammerItem] 建造了自己的第一所房屋，但是随着技术的进步，越来越多的建筑工具变得可用，使用时消耗的卡路里更少。使用[CraneItem] 可以快速完成大型建设项目，但要付出创建[Pollution] 的代价。

trans-unit.source: Types of Vehicles
trans-unit.target: 车辆种类

trans-unit.source: Types of Civic Objects
trans-unit.target: 公民对象的类型

一旦通过[Constitution]，新的公会政策即作为其创建的公会建筑物中的公民对象包含在内。公会建筑物的类型很多，每种类型都有其自己的公民对象类型：-[Laws] 设置在将[CourtItem]-[Demographics] 设置为[CensusBureauItem]-将[Districts] 设置为[ZoningOfficeItem]-将[Elected Titles] 设置为[GovernmentOfficeItem]-将[Civic Articles] 设置为[CapitolItem] 或[AmendmentsItem]-[Election Processes] 设置在[BoardOfElectionsItem]

trans-unit.source: Proposing Civic Objects
trans-unit.target: 提出公民对象

trans-unit.source:
Clicking the "Draft" button will allow a citizen to propose a new civic object. Citizens can optionally begin a new draft to a civic object by clicking the "Copy" button which appears when hovering over the icon. This will bring up a dialog allowing you to select any civic object (past or present) to use as a basis.
By visiting the Civic Object Component on a government building that already contains civic objects, these policies can be amended or repealed. To repeal a civic object, click the red 'X' that appears when you mouse over the icon. To amend an existing one, click the "Revise" button available in the interface.
The buildings containing civic objects must remain placed and active for the civic object to stay in effect. If a government building is picked up, all civic objects contained on it come with it and they will eventually become disabled if the building is not replaced.
trans-unit.target: 单击草稿“”按钮将允许公民提出新的公民对象。公民可以选择通过将鼠标悬停在该图标上时出现的“复制”按钮来开始针对公民对象的新草稿。这将弹出一个对话框，允许你选择任何公民对象（过去或现在）用作基础。

trans-unit.source:
- Apply Changes Immediately: Citizens given executive privilege can add, remove, and edit civic objects immediately.
- Propose via Election: Citizens with the ability to propose elections will be able to propose an addition/deletion/edit, which will then trigger a new election (using whatever {Election Process|Election Processes} that is defined in the constitution).
- View Only: If the citizen has no privileges as defined in the constitution, they may only view the civic objects by clicking on them and are not allowed to make changes.
trans-unit.target:
- 立即应用更改：被赋予行政特权的公民可以立即添加、删除和编辑公民物品。
- 通过推选提议：拥有推选提案能力的公民可以提出增加/删除/编辑，然后触发新的推选（使用公约中定义的任何[Election Process|Election Processes]。
- 只能查看：如果公民没有公约中定义的权限，则只能通过点击公民物品来查看，不允许进行修改。

trans-unit.source:
This component is accessed from a {RealEstateDeskItem}. It provides links to all of the individual deeds that can be accessed by interacting with a {ClaimToolItem} on a deed the citizen owns.
